description: Iraqi Shia Islamist political party and paramilitary organization, formed in 1982 as the military wing of the Supreme Council for Islamic Revolution in Iraq (SCIRI). Based in Iran during the Iran–Iraq War, it fought the Ba'athist regime of Saddam Hussein. After the 2003 US-led invasion, it integrated into Iraqi security forces and later became a political party. It is part of the Popular Mobilization Forces and has been active against ISIL.

country_links.evidence_quote: The organization was formed in Iran in 1982 as the military wing of the Supreme Council for Islamic Revolution in Iraq . It was based in Iran for two decades during the rule of Saddam Hussein and led by Iranian officers.
country_links.context: The organization was formed in Iran, based there for two decades, armed and directed by Iran, and deployed from Iran to Iraqi Kurdistan.

country_links.evidence_quote: Badr Brigade forces, and their Iranian commanders, have come to prominence in 2014 fighting the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) in Iraq.
country_links.context: The organization operates in Iraq, fighting ISIL, joining Iraqi security forces, and accused of sectarian killings and running secret prisons.
